---
title: "*Show Some Flash and Dew the Dew with a Little Bubbly!* (Graphical Dew, Bubble, and Flash) Reference Page"
author: "DOFPro group"
format: html
html-math-method: mathjax
---
::: {.callout-important title="Still Under Construction!"}
This web page is just a bare outline. The complete details have not been filled in yet.
:::
# Intro to *Show Some Flash and Dew the Dew with a Little Bubbly!*

Main title explanation

## Show Some Flash and Dew the Dew with a Little Bubbly!

Video 1 explanation

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/shKvpcsJIEM?si=6HOxLCfvXxPqVQRx" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>
[Visuals](../../NotYetHere.qmd)

# Examples and Definitions

## Examples Links

## Definitions

Dew Pressure
: The pressure at which a vapor of a given composition and temperature begins to condense. The composition of the condensate is usually also calculated.

Bubble Pressure
: The pressure at which a liquid of a given composition and temperature begins to evaporate. The composition of the vapor is usually also calculated.

Dew Temperature
: The temperature at which a vapor of a given composition and pressure begins to condense. The composition of the condensate is usually also calculated.

Bubble Temperature
: The temperature at which a liquid of a given composition and pressure begins to evaporate. The composition of the vapor is usually also calculated.

Flash
: A mixture with a known overall composition is brought to a temperature and pressure in the two-phase region. The mixture separates into a vapor phase and a liquid phase. The compositions of the two phases and their relative amounts are usually calculated.

Azeotrope
: The composition at which a mixture of two or more species has the same composition in the vapor phase as in the liquid phase at a specific temperature and pressure.

# Other Links and Videos

## Other Links

## Previous and Following Videos

### The video previous to the previous video (i.e., two videos back)

**Can You Tell an Azeotrope from a Peritectic? Get Your Phase Diagrams Right! Part 1**<br/>*Phase Diagrams, Gibbs Phase Rule*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/NUS4Y_pnWZk), [Info Page](../../balances/material/ReadingPhaseDiagramsVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/material/70-IntroMultiphasePart1Visuals.qmd)

### The previous video (i.e., one video back)

**Can You Tell an Azeotrope from a Peritectic? Get Your Phase Diagrams Right! Part 2**<br/>*Ternary Phase Diagrams, Lever Rule*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/QBREikNSvGI), [Info Page](../../balances/material/ReadingPhaseDiagramsVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/material/71-IntroMultiphasePart2Visuals.qmd)

### The next video (i.e., one video forward)

**Oh, Henry!**<br/>*Gas-Liquid Equilibrium with one condensible component*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/dCOQy1C7yf0), [Info Page](../../balances/material/HenrysLawVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/material/73-Visuals.qmd)

### The next after next video (i.e. two videos forward)

**Dew Your Bubbles Have Flash? Part 1**<br/>*Raoult's law, BUBL P, DEW P*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/vSkSRi6HYVQ), [Info Page](../../balances/material/RaoultsLawDewBubbleFlashVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/material/74-Visuals.qmd)
